tool.name: Scop3P
tool.description: fetches post-translational modifications (PTMs) from Scop3P database
tool.command: scop3p --accession '${accession}' --no-header --save modifications:tsv:'$modifications' #if str($include_peptides) == "true": --include-peptides --save peptides:tsv:'$peptides' #end if #if str($include_structures) == "true": --include-structures --save structures:tsv:'$structures' #end if #if str($include_mutations) == "true": --include-mutations --save mutations:tsv:'$mutations' #end if #if str($include_logs) == "true": --log-file '$logs' #end if

validator.message: UniProtKB accession numbers consist of 6 or 10 alphanumerical characters in the format '[OPQ][0-9][A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9]|[A-NR-Z][0-9]([A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}[0-9]){1,2}'
UniProtKB accession numbers consist of 6 or 10 alphanumerical characters

param.name: include_peptides
param.label: Include Phospho-peptides table
param.help: This table will show all available phospho-peptides identified by re-processing different phospho-proteome experiments for the particular protein of interest.

param.name: include_structures
param.label: Include Structures table
param.help: This table will list all available structures and the P-sites mapped onto the structure. By default the structures are ordered based on the number of P-sites they has and then by resolution.

param.name: include_mutations
param.label: Include Mutations table
param.help: This table lists amino acid variants and associated disease annotations mapped to available PDB structures.

param.name: include_logs
param.label: Include JSON log file
param.help: Generate an additional JSON log file with details of the tool execution.

organization.name: Bio2Byte, Vrije Universiteit Brussel (VUB)
organization.address: Interuniversity Institute Bioinformatics Brussels, Université Libre de Bruxelles, 1050 Ixelles, Brussels, Belgium

organization.name: CompOmics, Department of Biomolecular Medicine, Ghent University, VIB-UGent Center for Medical Biotechnology, VIB

tool.help:
`Scop3P <https://iomics.ugent.be/scop3p>`_ provides a unique and powerful resource to explore and understand the impact of phospho-sites on human protein structure and function, and can thus serve as a springboard for researchers seeking to analyse and interpret a given phospho-site or phosphoprotein in a structural, biophysical, and biological context.
The resource re-uses public domain data from a variety of leading international resources, including UniProtKB and PDB, but also uses reprocessed mass spectrometry-based phospho-proteomics data from *PRIDE* / *ProteomExchange*, which is in turn globally collected and thus wholly international-driven.
This tool downloads UniProt post-translational modification (PTM) annotations from the `Scop3P REST API <https://iomics.ugent.be/scop3p/swagger-ui/index.html>`_.
**Tool Parameters:**
- **UniProtKB Accession:** The UniProtKB accession number (6 or 10 alphanumerical characters). - **Peptides:** when selected, it gets peptides and modifications. - **Structures:** when selected, it gets structures and modifications. - **Mutations:** when selected, it gets mutations and modifications.
**About the Modifications table:**
This table will include the P-site information such as sequence position, modified residue and the source information (obtained from UniProtKB, PRIDE projects or both). For the P-sites obtained from UniProtKB, the evidence details such as if the site is identified by homologous sequence similarity (Similarity) or by experimental identification (Experimental) or combination of both (Combined) will be shown.
- "residue": `string` - "name": `string` - "evidence": `string` - "position": `int` - "source": `string` - "reference": `string` - "functionalScore": `float` - "specificSinglyPhosphorylated": `int`
**About the Phospho-peptide table (when 'Include Phospho-peptides table' is selected):**
This table shows all available phospho-peptides identified by re-processing different phospho-proteome experiments for the particular protein of interest. The number of projects column shows the occurrence of the particular peptide in different projects.
- "peptideSequence": `string` - "peptideStart": `int` - "peptideEnd": `int` - "peptideModificationPosition": `int` - "uniprotPosition": `int` - "score": `float` - "modifiedResidue": `string` - "evidence": `string` - "source": `string` - "functionalScore": `float` - "reference": `string`
**About the Structure table (when 'Include Structures table' is selected):**
This table will list all available structures and the P-sites mapped onto the structure. By default the structures are ordered based on the number of P-sites they has and then by resolution. For example here 3GOP has 6 P-sites and it is shown first in the table, while 3POZ and 3W33 have equal number of P-sites but are ordered based on the best resolution first.
Please note that some P-sites may not have a structure available, or they may be part of segments missing in the protein structure. You will not find such P-sites in this table, which only contains the sites which are seen in the observed segments of the protein structure. For example 3GOP chain A only contains P-sites 654,669,671,971,974,992 (e.g: 991 is in missing segment) and 3POZ chain A only contains 991,993,995,998,1016 (e.g 654,669,671 are not in modelled segment).
The method columns gives the structure determination method (X-ray, NMR etc) obtained from PDB, the stoichiometry - interfacing molecule/ligand and CSS scores are PISA predicted. PISA uses different features to calculate the most probable assembly of a particular structure (See Methods section to know how it is calculated and the article referenced in the paper) to determine the multi-meric state of the protein, which may not be the same as you see in PDB. CSS score ranges from 0-1 with 0 being less significant for the given complex (see Methods).
- "pdbId": `string` - "resolution": `float` - "stoichiometry": `string` - "interfacingMolecule": `string` - "method": `string` - "residue": `string` - "uniprotPosition": `int` - "secondaryStructure": `string` - "chainId": `string` - "pdbPosition": `int` - "accessibleSurfaceArea": `float` - "burriedSurfaceArea": `float` - "css": `float` - "conservedScale": `float`
**About the Mutations table (when 'Include Mutations table' is selected):**
This table lists amino acid substitutions reported for the queried protein, along with mapped PDB identifiers and disease association annotations.
- "position": `int` - "pdbIds": `list<str>` - "referenceAA": `string` - "altAA": `string` - "type": `string` - "disease": `string`
.. class:: infomark
**Scop3P** is developed at `Ghent University <https://www.ugent.be>`_ and is online since **June 2019**. This project is part of the `ELIXIR Belgium services <https://www.elixir-belgium.org/services>`_ (a curated repository of domain specific bioinformatics tools and resources for Plant and Biodiversity, Human Health and COVID-19 research, and general services to support research data management and data analysis in life sciences) after the review of ELIXIR Belgium's international Scientific Advisory Board.
.. class:: warningmark
For any further questions, feedback or suggestions, please send an email to: scop3p.compomics@vib-ugent.be.
